# UC-22.9.6 · Compliance Trending — SOC 2 Control Test Pass Rate vs Prior Quarter Baseline

## Description

Framework-specific trending shows whether SOC 2 Type II control tests are improving or eroding compared to your own prior quarter baseline—not just a static “percent pass today”—so leadership sees trajectory for trust services commitments.

## Value

Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for SOC 2, ISO 27001 compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes compliance and sourcetypes grc:control_test. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.

## Implementation

(1) Normalize `test_result` vocabulary via lookup; (2) align `period` to fiscal calendar if needed; (3) exclude duplicate tester reruns via `dedup control_id test_date`; (4) annotate dashboard with SOC subservice scope; (5) export quarterly screenshot for audit committee pack. Indexes required: `index=compliance`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=grc:control_test`.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk DB Connect (GRC export), or `index=compliance` HEC from Archer/ServiceNow GRC.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=compliance` `sourcetype="grc:control_test"` (framework, control_id, test_result, test_date, period_label).
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Normalize `test_result` vocabulary via lookup; (2) align `period` to fiscal calendar if needed; (3) exclude duplicate tester reruns via `dedup control_id test_date`; (4) annotate dashboard with SOC subservice scope; (5) export quarterly screenshot for audit committee pack.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=compliance sourcetype="grc:control_test" framework="SOC2" earliest=-400d
| eval pass=if(lower(test_result) IN ("pass","effective","no_exception"),1,0)
| eval test_epoch=strptime(test_date,"%Y-%m-%d")
| bin test_epoch span=90d as period
| stats avg(pass) as pass_rate by period
| sort period
| streamstats window=1 global=f latest(pass_rate) as prev_pass_rate by framework
| eval delta_pp=round(100*(pass_rate-prev_pass_rate),2)
| table period, pass_rate, prev_pass_rate, delta_pp
```

Understanding this SPL

**Compliance Trending — SOC 2 Control Test Pass Rate vs Prior Quarter Baseline** — Framework-specific trending shows whether SOC 2 Type II control tests are improving or eroding compared to your own prior quarter baseline—not just a static “percent pass today”—so leadership sees trajectory for trust services commitments.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=compliance` `sourcetype="grc:control_test"` (framework, control_id, test_result, test_date, period_label). **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk DB Connect (GRC export), or `index=compliance` HEC from Archer/ServiceNow GRC. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: compliance; **sourcetype**: grc:control_test. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=compliance, sourcetype="grc:control_test", time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **pass** — applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **test_epoch** — applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.
• Discretizes time or numeric ranges with `bin`/`bucket`.
• `stats` aggregates events by **period**, producing one row per unique combination for trend analysis and threshold evaluation.
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.
• `streamstats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by framework** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions (useful for per-host, per-user, or per-entity comparisons for this use case).
• `eval` defines or adjusts **delta_pp** — applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.

Enable Data Model Acceleration (and metric indexes for `mstats`) for the models or datasets referenced above; otherwise `tstats`/`mstats` may return no results from summaries.

Output fields: delta_pp, pass, pass_rate, period, prev_pass_rate, test_epoch.

Step 3 — Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with the GRC system, audit register, or framework crosswalk. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Line chart (pass_rate), Column chart (delta_pp), Single value (latest pass_rate).

## SPL

```spl
index=compliance sourcetype="grc:control_test" framework="SOC2" earliest=-400d
| eval pass=if(lower(test_result) IN ("pass","effective","no_exception"),1,0)
| eval test_epoch=strptime(test_date,"%Y-%m-%d")
| bin test_epoch span=90d as period
| stats avg(pass) as pass_rate by period
| sort period
| streamstats window=1 global=f latest(pass_rate) as prev_pass_rate by framework
| eval delta_pp=round(100*(pass_rate-prev_pass_rate),2)
| table period, pass_rate, prev_pass_rate, delta_pp
```

## Visualization

Line chart (pass_rate), Column chart (delta_pp), Single value (latest pass_rate).
